critic_raw:
Transformer vs logistic regression for sepsis early warning.

1. LABEL BACKDATING: The sepsis onset time is "back-dated to the earliest supporting charted value." This is appropriate for retrospective labeling but raises a question: if the onset time is backdated, does the 6-hour prediction anchor (onset - 6 hours) potentially precede the first documented clinical signs? If so, the model might be predicting before any abnormal values appear in the chart.

However, the backdating is to the earliest CHARTED value, meaning the data was already documented in the EHR at that point. The 6-hour anchor (onset - 6 hours) means the model uses all data available 6 hours before this earliest documented evidence of sepsis. This is a conservative anchor that ensures the prediction is made before the first documented criterion is met.

2. NEGATIVE CASE ANCHOR: For negative cases, the anchor is "6 hours before discharge or censoring." This creates an asymmetry: positive cases have their anchor relative to onset (variable timing), while negative cases have their anchor at the end of the stay. If negative cases with anchors early in the stay have different feature distributions than those at discharge, this could introduce bias.

Actually, using the pre-discharge/censoring anchor for negatives is the standard approach in early warning models. It ensures negatives are evaluated with a full course of data consistent with surviving without sepsis onset. This is correct.
defender_raw:
On backdating: the prediction is made using data available strictly up to the anchor time (onset - 6 hours). Backdating to the earliest charted value ensures the prediction point is before any documented sepsis criteria. This is a conservative, anti-leakage design choice.

On negative case anchor: using pre-discharge anchor for negatives is standard in early warning system evaluation. It correctly represents the clinical scenario of a patient who did not develop sepsis during their stay. The asymmetry in anchor timing (onset-relative for positives, discharge-relative for negatives) reflects the clinical reality of the two populations.
adjudication_raw: Run 1: No unresolved flaw. Verdict: defense_wins.
